failure_modes.text: Teams can call every preference a job and skip the work of separating true functional progress from surface wants or channel noise.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams call every preference a job
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate true functional progress from surface preferences: calling every want a job inflates the framework beyond its utility.

failure_modes.text: Analysis can stop at the obvious functional task and miss the deeper emotional or social job, leading to the wrong solution.
failure_modes.mitigation: Probe for the emotional and social dimensions behind the obvious functional task; stopping at the surface job leads to the wrong solution.

failure_modes.text: The team can solve the wrong problem by backing into the job from a preferred solution instead of from objective customer reality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Start from objective customer reality, not from a preferred solution; backing into the job from a product concept is motivated reasoning.

premortem_questions.text: What real progress is the user trying to make that feature comparisons alone are failing to explain?
premortem_questions.source_quote: real progress the user is trying to make

premortem_questions.text: What assumptions are we making about the job that would need to be true for this framing to hold up?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: If we ask why repeatedly, do we still land on the same job or do we uncover a deeper motivation?

heuristics.text: Define the job in terms of the outcome being hired for, not the product attribute being sold.
heuristics.source_quote: People don't want to buy a quarter-inch drill. They want a quarter-inch hole

heuristics.text: State the goal in ten words or less so the team cannot hide behind a feature-heavy story.
Use plain-language explanation before acting so the job stays clear and decision-relevant.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on identifying the real job, resisting feature obsession, and pressure-testing whether the team is solving the right problem.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader design-thinking sprawl because the raw source is strongest on outcome focus and anti-feature-first discipline.
open_questions: The first premortem item compresses several source passages on switching and adoption so the chunk stays concise.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on first-principles-thinking, critical-thinking, systems-thinking, and feynman-technique because those models clarify the real job behind the apparent solution.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on confirmation-bias and solution-specialization traps, but only clean current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source is clearer on ally and anti-pattern pressure than on explicit current-vocabulary tension blocks, so structured tensions should stay conservative.
